creator: erwin.vandevelde@gmail.com
text:
When postfix is being installed, it will overwrite the newaliases command
of sendmail (available in the base system). However, every time when the
base system gets reinstalled, this command is overwritten again with the
newaliases of sendmail. This is quite annoying for postfix users as the
commands act differently and newaliases suddenly does not change the
aliases database of postfix anymore.

Fix: 

Reinstall postfix. 
A better solution would be of course to ask the user during installation
of the base system or not overwriting newaliases when postfix is installed.
Requiring a full path specification of newaliases and putting it in different
locations is also a possible solution.
How-To-Repeat: Install postfix, and reinstall the base system afterwards.
Then execute newaliases to see that it is the one of sendmail
